tc_title: [BEAT][FV PM][AR] Validate RAPL=0 during OS Boot
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify package power is near-zero during OS boot — confirming RAPL PID is active from S0 entry.

HAS (NWP PM Features — Socket RAPL): "At first S0 entry PrimeCode activates the RAPL PID. Energy counters begin incrementing; power is expected low during boot (no workload)." Key assertion: socket_rapl_energy_status incrementing; pkg_power_consumed ≤ 10% TDP.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.
Boot stateCold boot (power-off); BIOS RAPL at defaults.
PythonSVsv.initialize() immediately after OS boot.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read TDP and power units.
import time
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
✅ PASS: TDP non-zero; units decoded.
❌ FAIL: pkg_tdp=0 → fuse not programmed; check BIOS reset sequence.
2Sample energy counter at T=0, wait 3 s, re-sample.
HAS formula: avg_power = (E2-E1)/(2^energy_unit)/delta_t
e1 = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
time.sleep(3.0)
e2 = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
if e2<e1: e2+=(1<<32)
boot_pwr = (e2-e1)/(2**en_unit)/3.0
print(f'Boot avg power={boot_pwr:.1f}W  (≤10% TDP={tdp_w*0.1:.1f}W expected)')
✅ PASS: e2 > e1 (RAPL active); boot_pwr ≤ 10% TDP.
❌ FAIL: e2==e1 → energy counter stuck; PID not initialized. Collect NLOG RAPL_INIT trace.
3Read pkg_power_consumed (PrimeCode decoded power float).
pwr = nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed.get_value()
print(f'pkg_power_consumed={pwr:.1f}W')
assert pwr < tdp_w*0.15, f'Boot power {pwr:.1f}W > 15% TDP'
✅ PASS: pkg_power_consumed ≤ 15% TDP — idle boot confirmed.
❌ FAIL: Power > 15% TDP → unexpected workload or RAPL over-reporting. Check PLR.

NOTEs

HAS: RAPL PID activates at S0 entry in Primecode reset handler.

Cold vs warm reset: at cold reset BIOS programs CSR PL1/PL2 from fuse defaults before PCode activates PID.

NWP path: sv.socket0.nio for all punit access.


Health Check — Registers & Logs to Collect

Register / LogAccessPass/Fail Criteria
socket_rapl_pl1_controlnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_controlReflects set PL1; lock bit = 0
perf_limit_reasonsnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasonsCorrect PLR bits asserted during throttle; clear after restore
pkg_power_consumednio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed≤ PL1 × 1.05 after convergence
NLOG RAPLpeg_client --nlog --filter RAPLNo error-level events; PID entries at ~1 ms cadence
socket_rapl_energy_statusnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_statusIncrementing from T=0; counter > 0 within 1 s of S0

Post-Process

Restore PL1 to TDP: cr.set_pl1_limit(tdp_w, sktNum=0, method='tpmi'). Collect NLOG if failed: peg_client --nlog --filter RAPL. Verify no residual PLR bits after restore.


References

  • NWP HAS PM — Socket RAPL: feature spec; convergence time formula (5×TW); PLR bit definitions
  • NWP PM MAS: TPMI field definitions; socket_rapl_pl1/pl2_control encoding
  • KB/pm_features/power_rapl/socket_rapl.md: PID loop rate; energy formula; Pm clamping
  • diamondrapids/pm/pmutils/cpu_rapl.py: set_pl1_limit() / set_pl2_limit()
  • pysvext.diamondrapids_flexcon.plugins.flexcon_pm: FlexconPM RAPL pre-checks

Validation Scope

Verify PCode applies the Cold TDP power limit offset when DTS temperature drops below the cold threshold.

HAS (NWP PM MAS — Cold TDP): "effective_PL1 = base_PL1 + cold_long_power_limit_delta when DTS < cold_long_power_limit_temp." Key assertion: pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0 increases by cold_delta after injection.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.
Thermal importimport diamondrapids.pm.Active_PM.Thermal_Management.CPU_Thermal_Management.thermal_debug as td
DTS injecttd.set_dts_temp() available.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read Cold TDP registers and base TDP.
import time
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
cold_tmp = nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.cold_long_power_limit_temp.get_value()
cold_dlt = nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.cold_long_power_limit_delta.get_value()
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  cold_thresh={cold_tmp}  cold_delta={cold_dlt/8.0:.1f}W')
✅ PASS: cold_dlt > 0 — Cold TDP feature initialized.
❌ FAIL: cold_dlt=0 → Cold TDP not enabled on this SKU; check BIOS Cold TDP knob.
2Record baseline effective PL1 (no temp injection).
base_budget = nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0.get_value()
print(f'Baseline effective PL1={base_budget/8.0:.1f}W')
✅ PASS: Baseline ≈ TDP ± 5%.
❌ FAIL: Budget far from TDP → pre-existing throttle; resolve before continuing.
3Inject cold DTS temperature (threshold − 10°C); wait 2 s; verify budget increases.
cold_inject = int(cold_tmp) - 10
td.set_dts_temp(cold_inject, imh_num_list=[0,1])
time.sleep(2.0)
cold_budget = nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0.get_value()
expected = base_budget + cold_dlt
print(f'Cold budget={cold_budget/8.0:.1f}W  expected={expected/8.0:.1f}W')
assert abs(cold_budget-expected) < 4, 'Cold TDP delta mismatch'
✅ PASS: Budget = base + cold_delta (within ±0.5W) — Cold TDP applied.
❌ FAIL: Budget unchanged → PCode not applying Cold TDP. Check nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.dts_temp. Collect NLOG COLD_TDP trace.
4Restore DTS; verify budget reverts.
td.set_dts_temp(40, imh_num_list=[0,1])
time.sleep(2.0)
restore = nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0.get_value()
print(f'Restored budget={restore/8.0:.1f}W  (expect~{base_budget/8.0:.1f}W)')
✅ PASS: Budget returns to baseline — Cold TDP deactivates correctly.
❌ FAIL: Budget stays elevated → Cold TDP hysteresis issue or DTS restore failed.

NOTEs

HAS (NWP PM MAS — Cold TDP): effective_PL1 = base + cold_delta when DTS < cold_thresh.

Units: power in U15.3 fixed-point (÷8 for Watts). Temperature in binary float.


Health Check — Registers & Logs to Collect

Register / LogAccessPass/Fail Criteria
socket_rapl_pl1_controlnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_controlReflects set PL1; lock bit = 0
perf_limit_reasonsnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasonsCorrect PLR bits asserted during throttle; clear after restore
pkg_power_consumednio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed≤ PL1 × 1.05 after convergence
NLOG RAPLpeg_client --nlog --filter RAPLNo error-level events; PID entries at ~1 ms cadence
cold_long_power_limit_tempnio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.cold_long_power_limit_tempNon-zero — threshold initialized
pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0Increases by cold_delta when DTS < threshold

Post-Process

Restore DTS: td.set_dts_temp(40, imh_num_list=[0,1]). style="margin:10px 0;">Restore PL1 to TDP: cr.set_pl1_limit(tdp_w, sktNum=0, method='tpmi'). Collect NLOG if failed: peg_client --nlog --filter RAPL. Verify no residual PLR bits after restore.


References

  • NWP HAS PM — Socket RAPL: feature spec; convergence time formula (5×TW); PLR bit definitions
  • NWP PM MAS: TPMI field definitions; socket_rapl_pl1/pl2_control encoding
  • KB/pm_features/power_rapl/socket_rapl.md: PID loop rate; energy formula; Pm clamping
  • diamondrapids/pm/pmutils/cpu_rapl.py: set_pl1_limit() / set_pl2_limit()
  • pysvext.diamondrapids_flexcon.plugins.flexcon_pm: FlexconPM RAPL pre-checks
  • NWP PM MAS — Cold TDP: cold_long_power_limit_temp / delta field definitions

Validation Scope

Verify that the NWP PrimeCode RAPL PID loop responds to a PL1 limit write within the HAS-specified convergence time (5 × configured time window τ), that measured package power converges to within ±5% of PL1, and that perf_limit_reasons bit[10] (PKG_PL1_INBAND) asserts correctly during throttle.

Micro-architecture: Socket RAPL enforces package power limits via a PID control loop in PrimeCode (NIO die). PL1 is the long-duration average power limit (τ = averaging window); PL2 is the short-term peak limit enforced as a near-instantaneous ceiling. The PID runs at ~1 ms intervals (PERIODIC_1MS) and distributes RAPL_PERF_LIMIT signals to CBBs via HPM 0x14. NWP topology: 1 NIO die + 2 CBBs.

Key registers used in this TC:

RegisterNWP namednodes pathPurpose
socket_rapl_power_unitsv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unitPower/energy/time scaling units
socket_rapl_pl1_controlsv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_controlPL1 limit value + enable + lock
socket_rapl_pl2_controlsv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl2_controlPL2 limit value + enable
socket_rapl_energy_statussv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_statusMonotonic energy counter (delta/time = avg power)
perf_limit_reasonssv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasonsActive throttle reason bitmask (PLR)
package_rapl_limit_cfgsv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_rapl_limit_cfgLegacy CSR PL1/PL2 (one-time BIOS setup)
package_power_skusv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_skuFused TDP, max power, min power
pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0sv.socket0.nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0Active PL1 budget after PCode processing
pkg_power_consumedsv.socket0.nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumedCurrent package power (decoded float, Watts)

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionPythonSV session active with NWP socket reachable (sv.socket0.nio responds without timeout)
WorkloadActive CPU workload running on all CBB cores — pkg_power_consumed must exceed target_pl1 (80% TDP) at test start to exercise the throttle path
RAPL unlockRAPL PL1 lock bit clear: socket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 — BIOS must not have locked limits
Python importsimport time and import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr importable in PythonSV session
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set in session for brevity

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read socket_rapl_power_unit and package_power_sku to decode power/energy/time units and TDP.

u       = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF
en_unit  = (u >> 6) & 0x1F

tdp_raw = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value()
tdp_w   = tdp_raw / 8.0
print(f'TDP = {tdp_w:.1f} W')
TDP reads non-zero; pwr_unit and en_unit decoded without exception.Exception reading registers → verify sv.socket0.nio.punit path is reachable.
2Sample socket_rapl_energy_status twice 2 s apart; compute average power.

e1 = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
time.sleep(2.0)
e2 = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
if e2 < e1: e2 += (1 << 32)
pre_pwr = (e2 - e1) / (2 ** en_unit)
print(f'Pre-limit avg power = {pre_pwr:.1f} W')
pre_pwr > 0 W and close to TDP — confirms workload is active and RAPL is initialized.pre_pwr ≈ 0 W → workload not running. pre_pwr >> TDP → RAPL loop not initialized; check PCode boot.
3Read socket_rapl_pl1_control bits[23:17] (TW field); decode tau and compute HAS-aligned timeout (5×tau + 100 ms margin).

pl1_raw     = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_control.read()
time_unit_s = 1.0 / (2 ** ((u >> 12) & 0xF))   # bits[15:12] -> s/LSB
tau_raw     = (pl1_raw >> 17) & 0x7F              # bits[23:17] = TW field
Y = tau_raw & 0x1F;  X = (tau_raw >> 5) & 0x3
tau_s       = (2 ** Y) * (1.0 + X / 4.0) * time_unit_s
timeout_s   = max(0.5, 5.0 * tau_s + 0.1)           # 5×tau + 100 ms margin
poll_n      = int(timeout_s / 0.1)                   # 100 ms polling intervals
print(f'tau = {tau_s*1000:.1f} ms  -> HAS timeout = {timeout_s*1000:.0f} ms')
tau_s and timeout_s printed. Typical: default tau ≈ 1 s → HAS timeout ≈ 5.1 s.tau_raw = 0 → PL1 not initialized; verify BIOS/PCode reset sequence completed (check BIOS POST log).
4Set PL1 = TDP × 0.8 using cr.set_pl1_limit(target_pl1, sktNum=0, method='tpmi'); start convergence timer.

target_pl1 = tdp_w * 0.8
cr.set_pl1_limit(target_pl1, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
t_start = time.time()
print(f'PL1 set to {target_pl1:.1f} W  (80% TDP)  — convergence timer started')
socket_rapl_pl1_control reflects new limit; no exception from set_pl1_limit.Exception from set_pl1_limit → TPMI interface unavailable; retry with method='msr'. Check socket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock = 0.
5Every 100 ms, sample socket_rapl_energy_status delta to compute windowed power. Stop when cur_pwr ≤ PL1 × 1.05 or timeout expires.

cur_pwr = None
for _ in range(poll_n):
    e1 = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
    time.sleep(0.1)
    e2 = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
    if e2 < e1: e2 += (1 << 32)
    cur_pwr = (e2 - e1) * 10 / (2 ** en_unit)   # 100 ms window -> Watts
    if cur_pwr <= target_pl1 * 1.05:
        t_settle = time.time() - t_start
        print(f'Converged to {cur_pwr:.1f} W in {t_settle*1000:.0f} ms')
        break
else:
    print(f'WARNING: Did not converge within {timeout_s*1000:.0f} ms '
          f'(5×tau = {tau_s*1000:.1f} ms); last = {cur_pwr:.1f} W')
✅ PASS: Power converges to ≤ PL1 × 1.05 within 5×tau window; convergence time printed.
❌ FAIL: Does not converge within timeout → RAPL PID not enforcing. Check HPM 0x14 (RAPL_PERF_LIMIT) delivery to CBBs via sv.sockets.cbbs.pcode.vars.*.
6Read perf_limit_reasons and verify bit[10] asserted during/after throttle.

plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR = 0x{plr:08X}  bit[10] PKG_PL1_INBAND = {bool(plr & (1<<10))}')
✅ PASS: bit[10] = True — PrimeCode generated PKG_PL1_INBAND throttle signal to CBBs.
❌ FAIL: bit[10] = False → PrimeCode not generating throttle; collect NLOG with peg_client --nlog --filter RAPL and inspect RAPL PID trace.

NOTEs

NWP register path: Always use sv.socket0.nio — NOT sv.socket0.imh0. CBB-side registers require sv.sockets.cbbs.cbb0.* / sv.sockets.cbbs.cbb1.*.

PLR bit decode: bit[9]=FAST_RAPL, bit[10]=PKG_PL1_INBAND, bit[11]=PKG_PL1_CSR, bit[12]=PKG_PL1_OOB, bit[13]=PKG_PL2_INBAND, bit[22]=RACL.

PID loop rate: 1 ms (PERIODIC_1MS in PrimeCode RaplHpmLeafFlowIO::init()). Iterations to settle ≈ 5 × tau / 1 ms.

Fast RAPL (PEM) convergence is ~10–15 ms (~10–20 PID cycles). This TC measures PL1 response, not Fast RAPL — the 5×tau bound applies here.

Minimum timeout: When tau ≤ 100 ms, the 500 ms floor applies regardless of the HAS formula.


Health Check — Registers & Logs to Collect

Register / LogAccessPass/Fail Criteria
socket_rapl_pl1_controlnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_controlPL1 enable bit = 1; PL1 value field matches target_pl1 (within 1 LSB); lock bit = 0
perf_limit_reasonsnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasonsbit[10] (PKG_PL1_INBAND) = 1 during throttle; all other PLR bits = 0 unless additional limiting active
socket_rapl_energy_statusnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_statusCounter monotonically incrementing (no stuck value); wraps at 32-bit correctly
pkg_power_consumednio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed≤ target_pl1 × 1.05 (within 5% of set limit) after convergence
NLOG / fw tracepmutil / peg_client --nlog --filter RAPLNo RAPL_ERR, MCA, or error-level NLOG events; RAPL PID entries visible at ~1 ms cadence

Post-Process

Restore PL1 to TDP: cr.set_pl1_limit(tdp_w, sktNum=0, method='tpmi'). If convergence failed (Step 5 timeout), collect a full NLOG trace: peg_client --nlog --filter RAPL and attach to the sighting. If PLR bit[10] never asserted (Step 6 fail), check CBB-side HPM delivery and file a sighting against PrimeCode RAPL with the NLOG excerpt and convergence timing data.


References

  • NWP HAS PM (NWP HAS PM Features) — RAPL feature list and convergence time specification
  • NWP PM MAS (NWP IMH SoC PM MAS) — RAPL TPMI register definitions and programming model
  • KB/pm_features/power_rapl/socket_rapl.md — Socket RAPL architecture deep-dive (local KB)
  • diamondrapids/pm/Active_PM/Power/cpuRAPL/socket_rapl_pmx.py — PMX reference: sweep, response, PL limit helpers
  • diamondrapids/pm/pmutils/cpu_rapl.py — set_pl1_limit() / set_pl2_limit() implementation
  • FlexconPM (pysvext.diamondrapids_flexcon.plugins.flexcon_pm) — RAPL pre-checks

Validation Scope

Verify RAPL enforcement across a sweep of PL1 values: TDP → 75% → 50% → 25% → Pm+10W. At each step PrimeCode PID must converge power to within ±5% of PL1 within 5×tau. Below Pm, PL1 is clamped to the Pm floor.

HAS (NWP PM Features — Socket RAPL): "Convergence time of 5 times the configured TW applies to any PL update." Below Pm: PCode clamps effective limit — cores cannot run below minimum frequency.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read TDP and Pm floor.
import time
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
min_pwr = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_min_pwr.get_value()/8.0
print(f'Pm floor={min_pwr:.1f}W')
✅ PASS: TDP and Pm decoded.
❌ FAIL: min_pwr=0 → Pm fuse not set.
2Sweep PL1 at TDP/75%/50%/25%; measure avg power after 6 s.
HAS: convergence within 5×tau (default tau≈1s → 5s timeout).
def measure(secs=2.0):
    e1=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
    import time; time.sleep(secs)
    e2=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
    if e2<e1: e2+=(1<<32)
    return (e2-e1)/(2**en_unit)/secs
for frac,lbl in [(1.0,"TDP"),(0.75,"75%"),(0.50,"50%"),(0.25,"25%")]:
    pl1=tdp_w*frac
    cr.set_pl1_limit(pl1,sktNum=0,method="tpmi")
    time.sleep(6.0)
    pwr=measure(2.0)
    ok=pwr<=pl1*1.05
    print(f'{lbl}: PL1={pl1:.0f}W pwr={pwr:.1f}W {"PASS" if ok else "FAIL"}')
✅ PASS: Power ≤ PL1×1.05 at each sweep point.
❌ FAIL: Power does not converge → check HPM 0x14 delivery; inspect NLOG RAPL PID trace.
3Pm floor test: set PL1 = Pm+10W; verify Pm clamping.
cr.set_pl1_limit(min_pwr+10,sktNum=0,method="tpmi")
time.sleep(6.0)
pwr_pm=measure(2.0)
print(f'Pm+10W: PL1={min_pwr+10:.0f}W  measured={pwr_pm:.1f}W  (may exceed PL1 - Pm clamping)')
✅ PASS: Power may slightly exceed programmed PL1 — Pm clamping prevents starvation (expected behavior).
❌ FAIL: Power far exceeds Pm+10W without clamping → Pm floor not enforced. Check pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0.
4Verify PLR bit[10] at lowest PL1 step.
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
assert plr&(1<<10), "PKG_PL1_INBAND not asserted"
✅ PASS: bit[10] SET — inband throttle to CBBs confirmed.
❌ FAIL: bit[10] clear → HPM 0x14 not delivered or PLR mux missing INBAND source.

NOTEs

HAS: 5×TW convergence. Default TW≈1s → 5s timeout.

Pm clamping: when PL1 < Pm, PCode clamps effective PL1 at Pm — power may exceed programmed PL1.

NWP path: sv.socket0.nio.


Health Check — Registers & Logs to Collect

perf_limit_reasonsnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasonsCorrect PLR bits asserted during throttle; clear after restore
pkg_power_consumednio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed≤ PL1 × 1.05 after convergence
NLOG RAPLpeg_client --nlog --filter RAPLNo error-level events; PID entries at ~1 ms cadence

Post-Process

Restore PL1 to TDP: cr.set_pl1_limit(tdp_w, sktNum=0, method='tpmi'). Collect NLOG if failed: peg_client --nlog --filter RAPL. Verify no residual PLR bits after restore.

  • NWP PM MAS: TPMI field definitions; socket_rapl_pl1/pl2_control encoding
  • KB/pm_features/power_rapl/socket_rapl.md: PID loop rate; energy formula; Pm clamping
  • diamondrapids/pm/pmutils/cpu_rapl.py: set_pl1_limit() / set_pl2_limit()

Validation Scope

Verify PCode correctly enforces the Pm (Minimum Power) floor — RAPL PL1 cannot throttle cores below their guaranteed minimum operating frequency.

HAS (NWP PM Features — Socket RAPL): "When PL1 is set below Pm, PCode clamps the effective limit at Pm. Cores floor at Pm ratio even if measured power exceeds the programmed PL1."

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.
pstatesDebugimport diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.pstatesDebug as pd importable.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read Pm ratio and TDP.
import time
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.pstatesDebug as pd
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
min_pwr = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_min_pwr.get_value()/8.0
pm_ratio = pd.get_pm_ratio(skt=0)
print(f'Pm floor={min_pwr:.1f}W  Pm ratio={pm_ratio}00MHz')
✅ PASS: Pm ratio non-zero — PrimeCode Pm floor initialized.
❌ FAIL: pm_ratio=0 → Pm not configured; check BIOS.
2Set PL1 = Pm/2 (well below Pm); run workload; verify core ratios floor at Pm.
cr.set_pl1_limit(min_pwr/2, sktNum=0, method="tpmi")
time.sleep(5.0)
for cbb in sv.sockets.cbbs:
    for core in list(cbb.computes.modules.cores)[:2]:
        ratio = (pd.debug.access_to_msr(0x198, core=core) >> 8) & 0xff
        print(f'{core.name}: ratio={ratio}00MHz (Pm={pm_ratio}00MHz)')
✅ PASS: Core ratios ≥ Pm ratio — Pm floor enforced even with PL1 < Pm.
❌ FAIL: Core ratios drop below Pm → Pm floor not being enforced; file P1 sighting.
3Verify PLR bit[10] and effective budget shows Pm clamping.
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
budget = nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0.get_value()/8.0

tc_title: Verify capability to change rapl limits to custom-defined ones
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify RAPL PL1/PL2 limits can be modified at runtime via TPMI, CSR, and OOB paths, and that each change takes effect immediately.

HAS (NWP PM MAS): TPMI path: socket_rapl_pl1_control bits[14:0] = PL1 value (U15.0 × power_unit). CSR path: package_rapl_limit_cfg. OOB path: PECI WrIAMSREx from BMC → PLR bit[12].

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read TDP and power units.
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
✅ PASS: TDP decoded.
❌ FAIL: Exception → SV session issue.
2Set PL1 = 200W via TPMI; read back.
cr.set_pl1_limit(200.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
pl1_back = (nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_control.read()&0x7FFF)/(2**pwr_unit)
print(f'TPMI PL1 readback={pl1_back:.1f}W  (expected~200W)')
assert abs(pl1_back-200.0)<2.0, f'TPMI PL1 mismatch: {pl1_back:.1f}'
✅ PASS: PL1 readback within ±2W of 200W — TPMI write confirmed.
❌ FAIL: Mismatch → TPMI write failed; check socket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock.
3Set PL1 = 150W via CSR (legacy BIOS interface); read back from TPMI.
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
cr.set_pl1_limit(150.0, sktNum=0, method='msr')  # CSR/MSR path
pl1_tpmi = (nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_control.read()&0x7FFF)/(2**pwr_unit)
print(f'After CSR write: TPMI readback={pl1_tpmi:.1f}W  PLR bit[11]=')
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
print(f'PKG_PL1_CSR bit[11]={bool(plr&(1<<11))}')
✅ PASS: TPMI reflects CSR write (within ±2W); PLR bit[11] = PKG_PL1_CSR asserts during throttle.
❌ FAIL: CSR write not reflected in TPMI → interface mux issue.
4OOB path: verify PLR bit[12] (PKG_PL1_OOB) is settable concept.
Note: OOB requires BMC/Redfish; mark as N/A if BMC not connected in this session.
# OOB verification (if BMC available):
cr.set_pl1_limit(180.0, sktNum=0, method='oob')  # OOB via PECI
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
print(f'PKG_PL1_OOB bit[12]={bool(plr&(1<<12))}')
✅ PASS: PLR bit[12] SET after OOB write — OOB path confirmed.
❌ FAIL: bit[12] clear → OOB not reaching PCode; check BMC connectivity.

NOTEs

HAS (NWP PM MAS): Three programmable paths: TPMI (inband), CSR (BIOS legacy), OOB (PECI/Redfish). PLR distinguishes source via bits[10/11/12].

Lock bit: if socket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock=1, writes are silently ignored.

Verify on PSS that PEM correctly reports PL1 throttle (PKG_PL1_INBAND, PLR bit[10]) after setting a restrictive PL1.

PSS Environment: VP (Simics) or HSLE. Validates PrimeCode RAPL behavior on pre-silicon model — finds firmware/model bugs before tape-out. Distinct from FV (silicon): Timing may differ; focus on PLR bit correctness and register values rather than exact convergence time.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Simics/HSLENWP PM model active; RAPL/PEM models initialized.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL modelPL1 lock=0; energy counter incrementing from T=0.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Set PL1=50W (well below TDP) via TPMI; wait 2s.
import time
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
nio = sv.socket0.nio
cr.set_pl1_limit(50.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
time.sleep(2.0)
✅ PASS: No exception; PL1 write accepted.
❌ FAIL: Exception → RAPL model not initialized in PSS.
2Read PLR; assert bit[10] = PKG_PL1_INBAND.
HAS (NWP PM Features): bit[10] = PKG_PL1_INBAND asserts when TPMI PL1 is the active throttle source.
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()

assert plr&(1<<10), 'PKG_PL1_INBAND not set'
✅ PASS: bit[10] SET — PSS PLR model correctly reports PL1 INBAND throttle.
❌ FAIL: bit[10] clear → PSS PLR mux not routing INBAND source; model bug; file FW/model sighting.
3Restore PL1 (300W); verify PLR clears.
cr.set_pl1_limit(300.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
time.sleep(2.0)
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
assert not (plr&(1<<10)), 'PLR bit[10] not cleared after restore'
✅ PASS: bit[10] clears after PL1 raised — PLR de-assert confirmed.
❌ FAIL: PLR sticks → PLR sticky bit not cleared; model issue.

NOTEs

HAS: PLR bit[10] = PKG_PL1_INBAND; only set when TPMI-programmed PL1 is throttle source.

PSS: convergence time may not match silicon; focus on PLR bit correctness.

❌ FAIL: Lock mismatch → BIOS knob not propagated to TPMI lock bit.

HAS: CSR PL1 = BIOS one-time setup. TPMI PL1 = runtime (overrides CSR at runtime). At boot: TPMI = CSR value.

tcd_title: Socket RAPL Registers Verification - CSR and TPMI
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify socket_rapl_energy_status counter increments monotonically and accurately computes average power.

HAS formula: avg_power_W = (E2 − E1) / (2^energy_unit) / delta_time_s
Counter wraps at 32 bits. energy_unit from socket_rapl_power_unit bits[12:8].

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read energy unit and sample counter over 5 s.
import time
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
e1=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
time.sleep(5.0)
e2=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
if e2<e1: e2+=(1<<32)
energy_j=(e2-e1)/(2**en_unit)
avg_w=energy_j/5.0
print(f'E1=0x{e1:X}  E2=0x{e2:X}')
print(f'Energy={energy_j:.2f}J  Avg power={avg_w:.1f}W')
✅ PASS: e2 > e1 (monotonic); avg_w plausible (10%–100% TDP at load).
❌ FAIL: e2==e1 → counter stuck; PID not running. e2 < e1 and no overflow → rollover not handled.
2Compare avg power from energy status with pkg_power_consumed direct read.
import time
pwr_direct = nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed.get_value()
print(f'Energy-derived avg={avg_w:.1f}W  Direct pkg_power={pwr_direct:.1f}W')
assert abs(avg_w-pwr_direct)<tdp_w*0.10, f'Energy-derived power diverges >10% TDP from direct read'
✅ PASS: Energy-derived power agrees with direct read within 10% TDP — energy counter accuracy confirmed.
❌ FAIL: Large divergence → energy unit mismatch or counter not tracking actual package power.
3Test 32-bit rollover handling.
# Sample near rollover (high energy workload):
e1=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
time.sleep(1.0)
e2=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
print(f'E1=0x{e1:08X}  E2=0x{e2:08X}  rollover_occurred={e2<e1}')
✅ PASS: Rollover handled correctly via (e2-e1+2^32) when e2 < e1.
❌ FAIL: Negative energy delta without rollover detection → measurement error.

NOTEs

HAS: avg_power = (E2-E1)/(2^energy_unit)/delta_t. Counter is monotonic, 32-bit, wraps.

energy_unit = bits[12:8] of socket_rapl_power_unit. Typical value 14 → 1/(2^14) J per LSB = 61µJ.

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read TDP, Pm, and max_power boundaries.
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr

import time

# [Manually set lock bit via raw write - platform specific]

print(f'PL1 after locked write attempt={pl1_check:.1f}W  (expect unchanged≈{tdp_w:.1f}W)')

desc:
Validation Scope

Verify PEM (PnP Excursion Monitor) correctly detects transient power excursions above PL2 and asserts PLR bit[9] (FAST_RAPL).

HAS (NWP PM Features — Fast RAPL): "PEM monitors power via SVID IMON at sub-ms intervals. PLR bit[9] (FAST_RAPL) asserts when PEM detects power > PL2. IMON × VID = instantaneous power." PEM convergence: ~2–5 PID cycles ≈ 10–15ms (much faster than PL1 5×tau).

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read TDP; set PL2 very low to trigger PEM excursion.
import time
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()

cr.set_pl2_limit(30.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
time.sleep(0.5)
✅ PASS: PL2=30W set — well below TDP; PEM will detect excursion when workload runs.
❌ FAIL: Exception → RAPL model issue.
2Assert PLR bit[9] = FAST_RAPL (PEM excursion detected).
HAS: bit[9] FAST_RAPL asserts when PEM detects IMON×VID > PL2.
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()

✅ PASS: bit[9] SET — PEM excursion monitor correctly detecting power > PL2.
❌ FAIL: bit[9] clear → PEM not detecting excursion; check SVID IMON path or PL2 encoding.

tcd_title: Socket RAPL Registers Verification - CSR and TPMI
tc_title: RAPL PL1/PL2 limits and Tau Verification
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify PL1/PL2 limit and tau (time window) registers can be set and read correctly via both TPMI and CSR paths, and that tau decoding matches the HAS formula.

HAS (NWP PM MAS — socket_rapl_pl1_control): tau = (2^Y) × (1 + X/4) × time_unit, where Y = bits[4:0], X = bits[6:5] of the time_window field. time_unit = 1/(2^time_unit_exponent) seconds.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read and decode PL1 and tau from TPMI.
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
pl1_reg=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_control.read()
pl1_val=(pl1_reg&0x7FFF)/(2**pwr_unit)
pl1_en=(pl1_reg>>15)&1
tau_raw=(pl1_reg>>17)&0x7F
time_unit_exp=(u>>12)&0xF
time_unit_s=1.0/(2**time_unit_exp)
Y=tau_raw&0x1F; X=(tau_raw>>5)&0x3
tau_s=(2**Y)*(1.0+X/4.0)*time_unit_s
print(f'PL1={pl1_val:.1f}W en={pl1_en}  tau={tau_s*1000:.1f}ms (Y={Y} X={X})')
✅ PASS: PL1 and tau decoded without exception; tau within [1ms, 100s] range.
❌ FAIL: tau_s = 0 or extremely large → tau_raw encoding error.
2Write custom PL1=175W and tau=500ms; read back; verify encoding.
# Target tau=0.5s: find Y,X such that (2^Y)*(1+X/4)*time_unit ≈ 0.5
# Simplest: Y=9, X=0 with time_unit_s = 1/1024 → tau ≈ 0.5s
cr.set_pl1_limit(175.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')  # set PL1
# Raw register write for tau (platform-specific helper may differ):
pl1_new=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_control.read()
pl1_rb=(pl1_new&0x7FFF)/(2**pwr_unit)
print(f'PL1 readback={pl1_rb:.1f}W  (expected~175W)')
assert abs(pl1_rb-175.0)<2.0, f'PL1 readback mismatch: {pl1_rb:.1f}'
✅ PASS: PL1 readback within ±2W of 175W — encoding correct.
❌ FAIL: Large mismatch → power_unit encoding wrong or write not taking effect.
3Read CSR PL1 and verify CSR/TPMI agree.
csr_pl1=(nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_rapl_limit_cfg.read()&0x7FFF)/(2**pwr_unit)
print(f'CSR PL1={csr_pl1:.1f}W  TPMI PL1={pl1_rb:.1f}W')
# CSR is BIOS default; TPMI is runtime override
print('Note: TPMI runtime overrides CSR. Different values are expected if BIOS set different default.')
✅ PASS: Both CSR and TPMI readable; values understood (CSR = BIOS, TPMI = runtime).
❌ FAIL: CSR = 0 → BIOS did not program CSR PL1; check BIOS knob.

HAS tau formula: tau = (2^Y) × (1 + X/4) × time_unit. Y=bits[4:0], X=bits[6:5] of time_window field.

time_unit_exponent = socket_rapl_power_unit bits[19:16]. Typical: time_unit_exp=10 → time_unit=1/1024s.

tc_title: RAPL Perf limit reasons - Fine & Coarse
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify RAPL PLR registers correctly show the active throttle source: bit[10]=PKG_PL1_INBAND (TPMI), bit[11]=PKG_PL1_CSR, bit[12]=PKG_PL1_OOB, bit[13]=PKG_PL2_INBAND.

HAS (NWP PM Features — PLR): Each PLR bit represents a distinct throttle source. Only the active source bit is set. Multiple bits can be simultaneously set if multiple sources are active.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Test INBAND throttle → bit[10].
import time
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
cr.set_pl1_limit(30.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
time.sleep(3.0)
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
assert plr&(1<<10), f'PKG_PL1_INBAND not set: PLR=0x{plr:08X}'
print(f'INBAND PLR=0x{plr:08X}  bit[10]={bool(plr&(1<<10))}')
cr.set_pl1_limit(tdp_w, sktNum=0, method='tpmi'); time.sleep(2.0)
✅ PASS: bit[10] SET for TPMI write; clears after restore.
❌ FAIL: bit[10] clear → INBAND source not propagating to PLR.
2Test CSR throttle → bit[11].
cr.set_pl1_limit(30.0, sktNum=0, method='msr')  # CSR path
time.sleep(3.0)
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
print(f'CSR PLR=0x{plr:08X}  bit[11]={bool(plr&(1<<11))}')
cr.set_pl1_limit(tdp_w, sktNum=0, method='msr'); time.sleep(2.0)
✅ PASS: bit[11] SET for CSR write; bit[10] clear (different source).
❌ FAIL: bit[11] clear but bit[10] set → TPMI overriding CSR; expected if TPMI value was lower.
3Test PL2 INBAND → bit[13].
cr.set_pl2_limit(30.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
time.sleep(1.0)
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
print(f'PL2 PLR=0x{plr:08X}  bit[13]={bool(plr&(1<<13))}')
assert plr&(1<<13), 'PKG_PL2_INBAND not set'
cr.set_pl2_limit(500.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
✅ PASS: bit[13] SET for PL2 TPMI write.
❌ FAIL: bit[13] clear → PL2 PLR source missing.

NOTEs

HAS PLR bit map: bit[9]=FAST_RAPL, bit[10]=PL1_INBAND, bit[11]=PL1_CSR, bit[12]=PL1_OOB, bit[13]=PL2_INBAND, bit[22]=RACL, bit[25]=PROCHOT.

Each source independently sets its PLR bit. Multiple sources can be simultaneously active.

Restore PL1 to TDP: cr.set_pl1_limit(tdp_w, sktNum=0, method='tpmi'). Collect NLOG if failed: peg_client --nlog --filter RAPL. Verify no residual PLR bits after restore.

tcd_title: Socket RAPL Registers Verification - CSR and TPMI
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0 accurately reflects the active PL1 budget after PCode processing — including Cold TDP adjustments and Pm clamping.

HAS (NWP PM MAS): pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0 is the PrimeCode-computed effective PL1. May differ from programmed PL1 due to Cold TDP, Pm clamping, or BIOS overrides. This is the actual power budget the RAPL PID uses.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Set PL1=TDP; read computed budget (expect ≈ TDP).
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
cr.set_pl1_limit(tdp_w, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
import time; time.sleep(2.0)
budget=nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0.get_value()
print(f'Programmed PL1={tdp_w:.1f}W  Computed budget={budget/8.0:.1f}W')
assert abs(budget/8.0-tdp_w)<2.0, 'Budget diverges from TDP at no-throttle'
✅ PASS: Computed budget ≈ TDP — no adjustment active at TDP.
❌ FAIL: Budget << TDP → pre-existing Cold TDP or Pm clamping adjusting the budget.
2Set PL1 = Pm/2 (below Pm); verify budget clamped to Pm.
min_pwr=nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_min_pwr.get_value()/8.0
cr.set_pl1_limit(min_pwr/2, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
time.sleep(2.0)
budget_pm=nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0.get_value()
print(f'PL1={min_pwr/2:.1f}W  Computed budget={budget_pm/8.0:.1f}W  (expect≈Pm={min_pwr:.1f}W)')
✅ PASS: Computed budget ≈ Pm floor — Pm clamping reflected in computed budget.
❌ FAIL: Budget equals programmed PL1 (below Pm) → Pm clamping not applied to computed budget.
3Set PL1=200W; verify budget reflects custom value.
cr.set_pl1_limit(200.0, sktNum=0, method='tpmi')
time.sleep(2.0)
budget_custom=nio.pcudata.pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0.get_value()
print(f'PL1=200W  Computed budget={budget_custom/8.0:.1f}W  (expect~200W)')
✅ PASS: Budget ≈ 200W — computed budget tracks TPMI PL1 correctly.
❌ FAIL: Budget unchanged → computed budget not updating on TPMI write.

NOTEs

HAS: pkg_computed_pl1_power_budget_0 = effective PL1 after all PCode adjustments.

Check this register when debugging: if power exceeds budget, RAPL PID feedback path has an issue.

tcd_title: Socket RAPL Registers Verification - CSR and TPMI
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify Socket RAPL OOB (Out-of-Band) power limit write via Redfish/PECI from BMC. PLR source: OOB write sets PLR bit[12] = PKG_PL1_OOB.

HAS (NWP PM MAS — OOB path): OOB path: BMC → Redfish API → PECI RdIAMSREx/WrIAMSREx → NIO PCode → TPMI. PLR bit[12] = PKG_PL1_OOB when OOB-sourced PL1 is the active throttle source.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.
BMCBMC reachable; Redfish API accessible from test host.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Verify OOB path via PECI write (platform helper or raw Redfish command).
import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
nio = sv.socket0.nio
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
pwr_unit = u & 0xF;  en_unit = (u >> 6) & 0x1F
tdp_w = nio.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_power_sku.pkg_tdp.get_value() / 8.0
print(f'TDP={tdp_w:.1f}W  pwr_unit={pwr_unit}  en_unit={en_unit}')
cr.set_pl1_limit(180.0, sktNum=0, method='oob')  # OOB via PECI
import time; time.sleep(3.0)
✅ PASS: OOB write accepted; no exception.
❌ FAIL: Exception → OOB not available; check BMC connectivity and PECI bridge.
2Verify PLR bit[12] = PKG_PL1_OOB (distinct from INBAND bit[10]).
HAS: bit[12] = PKG_PL1_OOB. Set only when OOB is the active throttle source.
plr = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons.read()
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
print(f'PLR=0x{plr:08X}')
print(f'  OOB bit[12]={bool(plr&(1<<12))}')
print(f'  IB  bit[10]={bool(plr&(1<<10))} (expect 0 - not INBAND)')
✅ PASS: bit[12] SET; bit[10] clear — OOB source correctly distinguished from INBAND.
❌ FAIL: bit[10] set instead of bit[12] → PLR source mux not distinguishing OOB from INBAND.
3Verify TPMI PL1 reflects OOB write.
pl1_back=(nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_control.read()&0x7FFF)/(2**pwr_unit)
print(f'TPMI PL1 after OOB write={pl1_back:.1f}W  (expected~180W)')
✅ PASS: TPMI reflects OOB write within ±2W — OOB→TPMI propagation confirmed.
❌ FAIL: TPMI not updated → OOB write not reaching PCode; check PECI bridge layer.

NOTEs

HAS: PLR bit[12]=PKG_PL1_OOB. Distinct from bit[10]=INBAND and bit[11]=CSR.

OOB path latency higher than INBAND — PLR may take 1–2s to assert after OOB write.

socket_rapl_pl1_controlnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_pl1_controlReflects set PL1; lock bit = 0
perf_limit_reasonsnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasonsCorrect PLR bits asserted during throttle; clear after restore
pkg_power_consumednio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed≤ PL1 × 1.05 after convergence

  • pysvext.diamondrapids_flexcon.plugins.flexcon_pm: FlexconPM RAPL pre-checks
  • Redfish API: /Systems/1/Bios/Actions — OOB power limit endpoint (varies by BMC implementation)

Validation Scope

Verify SVID IMON (Current Monitor) addressing and telemetry — RAPL uses IMON for fast power measurement via VR telemetry.

HAS (NWP PM MAS — SVID): IMON provides sub-ms current samples to PrimeCode. PrimeCode computes instantaneous power as IMON × VID. This feeds both Fast RAPL (PEM) and standard RAPL power tracking. IMON VR address must match the NWP SVID bus topology.

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
SV sessionsv.socket0.nio reachable without timeout.
Importsimport time; import diamondrapids.pm.pmutils.cpu_rapl as cr
Namespacenio = sv.socket0.nio alias set.
RAPL unlocksocket_rapl_pl1_control.pl1_lock == 0 (BIOS must not lock limits).
Platform S0Fully booted; no pending MCA.
SVID importimport diamondrapids.pm.Active_PM.Power.SVID.svid as svid importable.

Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Read SVID IMON VR address configuration.
import diamondrapids.pm.Active_PM.Power.SVID.svid as svid
nio = sv.socket0.nio
try:
    imon_addr=svid.get_imon_address(0)
    print(f'IMON VR address=0x{imon_addr:02X}')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'SVID IMON read: {e}')
✅ PASS: IMON VR address readable and non-zero — VR telemetry path configured.
❌ FAIL: Exception or address = 0 → SVID IMON not configured; check VR topology.
2Compare pkg_power_consumed at idle vs under load.
HAS: instantaneous power = IMON × VID. pkg_power_consumed = PrimeCode decoded IMON telemetry.
import time
idle_pwr=nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed.get_value()
print(f'Idle power={idle_pwr:.1f}W')
# [Start CPU stress workload externally]
time.sleep(5.0)
load_pwr=nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed.get_value()
print(f'Load power={load_pwr:.1f}W')
assert load_pwr>idle_pwr*1.5, f'Load power {load_pwr:.1f}W not significantly above idle {idle_pwr:.1f}W'
✅ PASS: load_pwr >> idle_pwr — IMON telemetry tracking power accurately.
❌ FAIL: load_pwr ≈ idle_pwr → IMON not responding to current changes; SVID telemetry gap.
3Verify energy counter delta is consistent with pkg_power_consumed.
e1=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
time.sleep(2.0)
e2=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_status.read()
if e2<e1: e2+=(1<<32)
u = nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
u=nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_power_unit.read()
en_unit=(u>>6)&0x1F
energy_avg=(e2-e1)/(2**en_unit)/2.0
direct=nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumed.get_value()
print(f'Energy-derived={energy_avg:.1f}W  Direct={direct:.1f}W')
assert abs(energy_avg-direct)<direct*0.15, 'IMON/energy counter diverge >15%'
✅ PASS: Energy counter and pkg_power_consumed agree within 15% — IMON telemetry consistent.
❌ FAIL: Large divergence → IMON address misconfigured; energy unit mismatch; or SVID bus issue.

NOTEs

HAS (NWP PM MAS — SVID): IMON × VID = instantaneous power. PrimeCode accumulates for RAPL energy tracking.

SVID bus topology: NIO die connects to VR via dedicated SVID bus. IMON address must match the VR type.


Health Check — Registers & Logs to Collect

pkg_power_consumednio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumedIncreases significantly under load vs idle
socket_rapl_energy_statusnio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.socket_rapl_energy_statusConsistent with pkg_power_consumed (within 15%)

Validation Scope

This TC verifies that DRAM RAPL is disabled in NWP.

HAS Ref — ZBB Negative Checks (Zero-Bug-Bounce): ZBB negative validation exercises error paths to confirm PCode gracefully handles out-of-spec inputs without MCA or hang. Covered domains: DRAM RAPL negative (invalid PL values), Memory PM negative (bad DIMM state requests), PKGC negative (forced entry with blocking conditions), Platform RAPL/PSYS negative (out-of-range power limits), SST-PP/CP/BF negative (invalid profile or bucket selects). Expected: PCode clamps or rejects invalid values; NLOG records the event; no fatal MCA or system hang.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Feature[PSS]DRAM RAPL ZBB Negative Checks
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Configure DRAM RAPL feature with an out-of-spec / invalid parameter (e.g., power limit below minimum, invalid profile index).System accepts input; PCode clamps or rejects value gracefully; no immediate MCA or hang.
2Verify PCode NLOG records the invalid-parameter event with appropriate error code or warning.NLOG entry present; error code matches expected DRAM RAPL rejection path.
3Verify system continues normal operation after invalid parameter attempt: PM state transitions, C-states, and power limiting still functional.System stable; normal PM operations unaffected after negative-test stimulus.
4Run the automation script.Automation PASS; DRAM RAPL ZBB negative checks verified.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • NLOG records invalid-parameter event with expected error code.
  • System remains stable and PM-functional after each negative test.
  • Package power within PL1 ± 5% across cross-product run duration.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

Aim of this TC is to make sure bios correctly configures root/leaf socket for Psys via B2P mailbox.

 

B2P mailbox command as defined in the Psys Pcode FAS

PSYS_CONFIG

Data Register:

31 - MASTER - Indicates this socket is a psys master. 1 = Master, 0 = Not-Master

30:8 - Reserved

7:0 - Node IDs mask of Sockets that are part of this Platform Psys Domain

Bit 0 - Socket 0

Bit 1 - Socket 1 etc.,

 

This command is sent to each socket that is the master.

A Socket can not be both master and slave.

B2P command should make sure the slave ids does not coincide with master id. 

It is legal to have 0 slaves for master. 

Send Data = 0 to Socket0 to clear out the default master behavior. - Optionally could send Data=0 to all non-master sockets.

Bios needs to send all sockets with this PSYS_CONFIG B2P command in order to clear the default behavior whether it is master (1) or slaves (0) socket.

 

As per HPM implementation in GNR, the Socket 0 would be always the root - hence the Test cases should be considering the HPM architecture and have all the above criteria for Test cases.

 

Verify BIOS knobs to make sure correct socket is configured as master. Also make sure slave sockets are correctly gets configured via B2P BIOS mailbox (PSYS_CONFIG). 

B2P Command called "PSYS_CONFIG"

  • Has 2 inputs : DATA[31:31] : IS_MASTER; 1= MASTER, 0 = SLAVE
                           DATA[7:0] : SLAVE_NID_MASK
  • PCODE variables populated : &PSYS_CONFIG_PSYS_DATA, &PSYS_CONFIG_PSYS_I_AM_MASTER and &PSYS_CONFIG_PSYS_SLAVE_NID_MASK.

 

Test steps (from GNR):

Use pm.pmutils.platform_rapl.py script functions: verify_psys_configuration

Expectation
1. The Root IMH Primecode populates the RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM message with minimum value PID Output between Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL and Psys and sets the corresponding bit indicating which amongst the three is the limiter to secondary IMH.
2. However the Leaf IMH Primecode populates the RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM message with minimum value PID Output between RACL, Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL and Psys and sets the corresponding bit indicating which amongst the four is the limiter to the CBB dies. DMR follows a dual hierarchy HPM infrastructure where the Root IMH sends the RAPL_PERF_LIMIT message to Leaf IMH and each of the IMH sends the RAPL_PERF_LIMIT message to their corresponding leaf CBBs.Note: As RACL is local to each IMH, RACL_PID_FREQ_OUTPUT=0xFF is used in HPM message from IMH-P to IMH-S.
3. The CBB Pcode is responsible to send out the LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM if any of the cores is affected by *RAPL or RACL.
4. The Leaf IMH Primecode is responsible for populating the RACL_PERF_STATUS register in the TPMI space as well as sending the aggregated LEAF_PERF_STATUS for its heirarchy.
5. The Root IMH Primecode is responsible for populating the PLATFORM_RAPL_PERF_STATUS and SOCKET_RAPL_PERF_STATUS register as well as its own RACL_PERF_STATUS

This TCD makes sure that the platform power resolves to the limits set in the ppl1/ppl2 registers

 

Registers to use:

platform_rapl_pl_info

platform_rapl_limit_cfg

platform_rapl_limit

 

Flow:

 

PPL1 limit check

  1. Enable platform RAPL through BIOS knobs
  2. Write to enable bit of platform_rapl_limit register
  3. Run a workload (TDP workload like ptu -ct 3)
  4. Write 0x0 to ppl1_limit 
  5. Read the platform power - this is min platform power 
  6. Reset the ppl1_limit to max value based on platform power info register/platform_rapl_pl_info register
  7. Read the platform power - this is max platform power 
  8. Select a random value for PPL1 between min power and max power. (PPL2 fixed/default Value)
  9. Read the platform power and ensure that the platform power measures the value set in PPL1 limit
  10. Reset ppl1_limit to max value based on platform power info register
  11. Read the platform power - this value should be same as max platform power
  12. Kill the workload

 

PPL2 limit check

  1. Enable platform RAPL through BIOS knobs
  2. Write to enable bit of platform_rapl_limit register
  3. Run a workload (TDP workload like ptu -ct 3)
  4. Write 0x0 to ppl2_limit 
  5. Read the platform power - this is min platform power 
  6. Reset the ppl2_limit to max value based on platform power info register/platform_rapl_pl_info register
  7. Read the platform power - this is max platform power 
  8. Select a random value for PPL2 between min power and max power. (PPL1 fixed/default Value)
  9. Read the platform power and ensure that the platform power measures the value set in PPL2 limit
  10. Reset ppl2_limit to max value based on platform power info register
  11. Read the platform power - this value should be same as max platform power
  12. Kill the workload 

 

Pass/Fail criteria:

Platform power should resolve to the value set in the ppl1/ppl2 registers else the test is a fail

tc_title: Verify capability to change rapl limits to custom-defined ones
desc:
Flow *

Case 1 (Decrease PPL1/PPL2 limits from the programmed one to lesser than default expected limits)

1. Using punit register, enable platform RAPL for PPL1 register

2. Calculate platform power by running Core x IO Traffic

3. Using punit register program PPL1 to calculated power (step 3) - 10W

4.  Using punit register program TW1 to 1sec

5. Run Core x IO Traffic for 15 mins.﻿

6. reboot the system

Case 2 (Increase PPL1/PPL2 limits from the programmed one to greater than default exptected values  (negative testing) )

1. Using punit register and ioreg register, enable platform RAPL for both PPL1 and PPL2  register

2. Program PPL1 and PPL2 to max value (say the max value the register can hold), Using both punit and ioreg registers.

3. Run Core x IO Traffic for 15 mins.

4. reboot the system

Case 3 (Set customer PPL1/PPL2 limits across the 2 interfaces: TMPI & CSR and make sure the resolved PL1/PL2 matches the most restrictive limit)

1.  Using punit register, enable platform RAPL for both PPL1 and PPL2 register.

2. Calculate platform power by running Core x IO Traffic

3. Using punit register program PPL1 to calculated power (step 3) - 10W

4.  Using punit register program TW1 to 1sec

5. Using punit register program PPL2 to 1.2 * PPL1

6. Using punit register program TW2 to 16ms.

7. Using ioreg register program PPL1 to calculated power (step 3) - 20W

8.  Using ioreg register program TW1 to 1sec

9. Using ioreg register program PPL2 to 1.2 * PPL1

10. Using ioreg register program TW2 to 16ms.

11. Run Core x IO Traffic for 15 mins.

12. reboot the system.


Case 4 (Set PPL1 limit > PPL2 limit  (negative testing) )

1. Using punit register, enable platform RAPL for both PPL1 and PPL2 register

2. Calculate platform power by running Core x IO Traffic

3. Using punit register program PPL2 to calculated power (step 3) - 10W

4.  Using punit register program TW2 to 16ms

5. Using punit register program PPL1 to 1.2 * PPL2

6. Using punit register program TW1 to 1s.

7. Run Core x IO Traffic for 15 mins.


Configuration *
For JOHNSONCITY boards no configuration is required. For the system with customized boards, based upon the sensor configured, make even/odd sockets set as root

For the system with JOHNSONCITY board

&
Disable CSR lock

 

Pass/Fail criteria *
For case 1
Platform power should converge to the programmed PPL1 limit
No hang or failures should be reported
For case 2
No hang or failures should be reported
For Case 3
The measured platform power should converge to the limits programmed in the ioreg register.
For Case 4

Platform power should converge to PPL2 limit.

No hang or failures should be reported

Validation Scope

This TC verifies that Platform RAPL is disabled in NWP.

HAS Ref — ZBB Negative Checks (Zero-Bug-Bounce): ZBB negative validation exercises error paths to confirm PCode gracefully handles out-of-spec inputs without MCA or hang. Covered domains: DRAM RAPL negative (invalid PL values), Memory PM negative (bad DIMM state requests), PKGC negative (forced entry with blocking conditions), Platform RAPL/PSYS negative (out-of-range power limits), SST-PP/CP/BF negative (invalid profile or bucket selects). Expected: PCode clamps or rejects invalid values; NLOG records the event; no fatal MCA or system hang.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Feature[PSS]Platform RAPL / PSYS ZBB Negative Checks
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Configure Platform RAPL/PSYS feature with an out-of-spec / invalid parameter (e.g., power limit below minimum, invalid profile index).System accepts input; PCode clamps or rejects value gracefully; no immediate MCA or hang.
2Verify PCode NLOG records the invalid-parameter event with appropriate error code or warning.NLOG entry present; error code matches expected Platform RAPL/PSYS rejection path.
3Verify system continues normal operation after invalid parameter attempt: PM state transitions, C-states, and power limiting still functional.System stable; normal PM operations unaffected after negative-test stimulus.
4Run the automation script.Automation PASS; Platform RAPL/PSYS ZBB negative checks verified.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • NLOG records invalid-parameter event with expected error code.
  • System remains stable and PM-functional after each negative test.
  • Package power within PL1 ± 5% across cross-product run duration.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • See Validation Scope for spec / bug references.

desc:

Flow *


1. In the root Die across all sockets, Enable platform RAPL using punit registers.

Expectation
1. The Root IMH Primecode populates the RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM message with minimum value PID Output between Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL and Psys and sets the corresponding bit indicating which amongst the three is the limiter to secondary IMH.
2. However the Leaf IMH Primecode populates the RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM message with minimum value PID Output between RACL, Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL and Psys and sets the corresponding bit indicating which amongst the four is the limiter to the CBB dies. DMR follows a dual hierarchy HPM infrastructure where the Root IMH sends the RAPL_PERF_LIMIT message to Leaf IMH and each of the IMH sends the RAPL_PERF_LIMIT message to their corresponding leaf CBBs.Note: As RACL is local to each IMH, RACL_PID_FREQ_OUTPUT=0xFF is used in HPM message from IMH-P to IMH-S.
3. The CBB Pcode is responsible to send out the LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM if any of the cores is affected by *RAPL or RACL.
4. The Leaf IMH Primecode is responsible for populating the RACL_PERF_STATUS register in the TPMI space as well as sending the aggregated LEAF_PERF_STATUS for its heirarchy.
5. The Root IMH Primecode is responsible for populating the PLATFORM_RAPL_PERF_STATUS and SOCKET_RAPL_PERF_STATUS register as well as its own RACL_PERF_STATUS


NOTE:
(cbbs do not have rapl pids directly, they are mapped to clos limits) without injection clos is high - with injection clos is updated to 0x4 
power limit in root socket should be reflected in leaf sockets (all other sockets in the platform should be updated with the same value)

the platform RAPL pid output across all dies in the sockets should be equal to 0x1

After disabling platform RAPL, via root die (imhP) across all sockets
the platform RAPL pid output across all dies in the sockets should be equal to p0
the socket rapl pid output across all dies in the sockets should be equal to p0

Verify the SVID address for VCCIN VR

Use below registers to verify the SVID telemetry and addressing are programmed properly


The registers that participate in configuring and controlling the telemetry data pulls from the VRs are as follows:

  • SVID_ADDR_CONFIG[15:0] - an array of 16 legacy registers used for configuring the logical VRs;
  • SVID_IFC_CONFIG[1:0] - an array of 2 legacy registers used for configuring the two SVID buses;
  • SVID_IMON_MASK - legacy register used for enabling/disabling I_Out (aka Imon) telemetry pulls from each of the logical VRs; if a specific VR represents a Psys device, the corresponding bit in this register enables/disables the I_In (instead of I_Out) telemetry pulls from the VR;
  • SVID_PWRIN_MASK - legacy register used enabling/disabling PwrIn telemetry pulls from each of the logical VRs.

In order to provide better accuracy VR14 supports telemetry data which are 16 bit wide instead of 8 bits on VR13. Psys will be using Register 1Bh of the primary Socket CPU0 to contain the Psys value.

  • Test steps:
  1. Check energy status at idle and make sure the power measured matches the expected power under idle conditions
  2. Run WL
  3. Check the energy status and make sure the power measured matches the expected value

 

  • Use pm.pmutils.psys.py script function:
  1. read_platform_energy_status 
  2. calc_average_platform_power_consumption

Registers to use:

CSR: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.platform_energy_status

TPMI: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.platform_rapl_energy_status

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 

Intent Verification Strategy 

- The aim of the TC is to validate the platform energy status reporting through CSR and TPMI interface

-Energy status will be reported by the primary socket only

-registers to check platform_rapl_energy_status,platform_energy_status

 


Flow

TPMI interface (platform_rapl_energy_status)

  1. Read energy status register - store as estatus1, time1
  2. Read energy status register again- ensure the value increments -store as estatus2,time2
  3. Calculate power at idle conditions (estatus2-estatus1)/(time2-time1)
  4. Run workload (core X io traffic) / (PTU TDP workload)
  5. Read energy status register - store as estatus1, time1
  6. Read energy status register again- ensure the value increments -store as estatus2,time2
  7. Calculate power with workload (estatus2-estatus1)/(time2-time1) 
  8. Compare the power value calculated to the pcodeiomap register thats polling the SVID data and primecode variable 
    1. sv.sockets.imh0.pcodeio_map.io_platform_energy_status_low , sv.sockets.imh0.pcodeio_map.io_platform_energy_status_high

 

CSR interface (platform_energy_status)

  1. Read energy status register - store as estatus1, time1
  2. Read energy status register again- ensure the value increments -store as estatus2,time2
  3. Calculate power at idle conditions (estatus2-estatus1)/(time2-time1)
  4. Run workload (core X io traffic)/ PTU TDP workload
  5. Read energy status register - store as estatus1, time1
  6. Read energy status register again- ensure the value increments -store as estatus2,time2
  7. Calculate power with workload (estatus2-estatus1)/(time2-time1)

 

 

Pass/Fail criteria *

Energy status register should increment

power vale should match across all interfaces

Mark test as fail if register does not increment

desc:
(Sections with * are mandatory. You must fill them in, or say "Not Applicable" or "N/A" if you have seriously considered it and determined there's nothing to say. Remove these instructions and the explanatory text in each section as you fill things in.)

Flow *

Describe HOW the TCD will be tested. Accurate and complete instructions how to run the test, including command line (if applicable). This should not be a duplication of the Test Content record, if one is created.


Make sure that under platform rapl power limit condition the corresponding PEM_status and PEM_Counters are set/incremented.

Based on the PEM HAS (https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/Wave3_common/PEM/PEM_HAS.html), the status bits & counters of interest for platform rapl are highlighted below:






Initial setup:

  1. Enable all the required BIOS knobs for Platform RAPL and boot up the system 
  2. Read the Enable_PEM bit from PEM Control Register and enable PEM on all the sockets -
    • PEM has to be enabled in all the sockets
    • If PEM is not enabled in any instance of any socket. Mark the test as Fail
  3.  Read the SSE TRL frequency for the corresponding system
    1. Reading the SSE TRL frequency of the system via SST_PP_INFO_4
  4. Set the Frequency Excursion Threshold (FET) present in PEM Control Register to SSE TRL on all sockets.
  5. Tune sensitivity of excursion reporting using TW present in PEM Control Register on all sockets 
  6. Read the Frequency Excursion Threshold and PEM Time Window on all sockets
  7. Calculate Min Power and Workload Power
    1. Choose a power limit which will be a median of Min Power and Workload Power
  8. Run a TDP workload.

 

Checks for TPMI interface

  1. Change PPL1 for all sockets via TPMI
  2. Run a TDP workload 
  3. *toDo add PLR checks - read PLR mailbox - tpmi plr die level register- plr-mailbox (coarse and fine) - ensure all bits related to platform RAPL are set *
  4. From PEM Status register ensure the Bit 16 "PEM_PLATFORM_PL1_MSR_TPMI" is set
  5. platform power should be equal to the value set in PPL1 register 
  6. Stop the TDP workload and set back the PPL1 limit to Default.
    • Default Value will be mention in the PLATFORM_RAPL_PL_INFO[17:0]
  7. Reset the PEM_STATUS & then read back PEM Status register ensure that we are not throttled by "PEM_PLATFORM_PL1_MSR_TPMI"
  8. Change PPL2 values for all sockets via TPMI
  9. Run a TDP workload.
  10. From PEM Status register ensure the Bit 19 "PEM_PLATFORM_PL2_MSR_TPMI" is set
  11. Stop the TDP workload and set back the PPL2 limit to 1.2*Default
    • Default Value will be mention in the PLATFORM_RAPL_PL_INFO[53:36]
  12. Reset the PEM_STATUS & Read back PEM Status register ensure that we are not throttled by "PEM_PLATFORM_PL2_MSR_TPMI"

 

Checks for CSR interface

  1. Change PPL1 for all sockets via CSR
  2. Run a TDP workload.
  3. From PEM Status register ensure the BIT 17 "PEM_PLATFORM_PL1_MMIO" is set
  4. platform power should be equal to the value set in PPL1 register  
  5. Stop the TDP workload and set back the PPL1 limit to Default
    • Default Value will be mention in the PLATFORM_RAPL_PL_INFO[17:0]
  6. Reset & Read back PEM Status register ensure that we are not throttled by "PEM_PLATFORM_PL1_MMIO"
  7. Change PPL2 for all sockets via CSR
  8. Run a TDP workload.
  9. From PEM Status register ensure the BIT 20 "PEM_PLATFORM_PL2_MMIO" is set
  10. Stop the TDP workload and set back the PPL2 limit to 1.2*Default
    • Default Value will be mention in the PLATFORM_RAPL_PL_INFO[53:36]
  11. Reset & Read back PEM Status register ensure that we are not throttled by "PEM_PLATFORM_PL2_MMIO"

Aim of this TC to validate the perf status fucntionality i.e. on a rapl power limited system making sure that the perf status register is incrementing.

Link to related wave3 common Psys HAS: https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/Wave3_common/Psys/10nm%20wave%203%20Psys_platform%20turbo%20HAS%20rev0.98.html#platform_rapl_socket_perf_status

Impact of HPM on this register from GNR HAS: https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/GNR/Features/psys/gnr_psys.html#hpm-impact-to-platform_rapl_socket_perf_status

 

 

  • Test steps:
  1. Under idle condition make sure the perf status register is not incrementing
  2. Run WL (eg PTU TDP WL) and set the rapl PL1 limit before TDP to cause a rapl power limit condition

Eg steps from GNR:

 Run TDP workload --> ptu -ct 1 -mon -log -csv -t 120 [TDP Workload]

 Set the PPL1 limit to minimal using this command - pr.set_ppl1_limit(limit=100)

  1. Check the perf status register and make sure it is incrementing
  2. Set PPL1 back to default value & stop the WL, make sure the bits are re-set back to zero
  3. Repeat step 2-4 for by varying PPL2 limit as well

 

Also within the pmx test section "check_platform_rapl_perf_status_validity" makes sure the perf status is functioning as expected.


Register of interest:

sv.socket0.imh0.uncore.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_rapl_perf_status

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 

The aim of the TC is to validate the platform perf status reporting through CSR and TPMI interface

 

Registers to use:

platform_rapl_perf_status

platform_rapl_limit_cfg.ppl1

platform_rapl_limit_cfg.ppl2 


Flow 

  1. Enable platform RAPL through bios knobs
  2. Read the perf_status register under idle conditions - it should not increment 
  3. Run workload (PTU TDP workload) ./ptat -ct 3 
  4. Set ppl1 limit to minimum value
  5. Read the perf status register and make sure it is incrementing 
  6. Set ppl1 back to default value 
  7. Stop workload 
  8. Ensure that perf status does not increment 
  9. Repeat step 2-5 with ppl2 

 

Pass/Fail criteria *

Perf_status register should not increment in idle condition

With active workload and platform rapl limited , perf status register should increment

Once the workload is killed, perf_status register should be static

socket0.imh1.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.platform_rapl_perf_status - 0x0000000000000000

socket0.imh0.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.platform_rapl_limit - 0x0000000000000000

desc:
The aim of the TC is to validate the platform power info register. Link to related HAS from wave3 common Psys HAS: https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/Wave3_common/Psys/10nm%20wave%203%20Psys_platform%20turbo%20HAS%20rev0.98.html#platform_power_info



 

  • Checkout list:
  1. Make sure the default value of the register is as expected
  2. Enable or Disable Platform Power Info Lock 
  3. Program max PPL1/PPL2 limit & Min PPL1 limit
  4. Program max PPL time widow
  5. For each of the above test step: Verify that programming takes into effect and algorithm takes action based on updated limits

 

 

  • Use pm.pmutils.psys.py script functions:

 

enable_platform_power_info_lock

disable_platform_power_info_lock

read_max_ppl1_limitq

read_max_ppl2_limit

set_max_ppl1_limit

set_max_ppl2_limit

read_min_ppl1_limit

set_min_ppl1_limit

 

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 

Registers to use:

platform_power_info_cfg


 

Flow:

  1. Enable platform rapl through bios knobs 
  2. Check for default value programmed in the register based on bios knob settings
  3. Set Platform RAPL limit&info bios knob to manual

  4. Manually set the limits in the bios knobs

 

5. Check whether the value is reported in platform power info register

Pass/Fail criteria:

Platform power info register should reflect the value as per the bios knob selection in manual and all other modes

socket0.imh1.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.platform_power_info - 0x0000000000000000

0x15fff80001fff8


Bios knobs:

Psys manual setting

 

 PsysPowerLimitCsrLock=0x0,PsysPowerInfoLock=0x0,PsysPowerLimitAndInfo=0xFF",

 PsysPowerLimit1En_0=0x1,PsysPowerLimit1Power_0=0x3200,PsysPowerLimit1Time_0=0xA #Psys socket0 PPL1 configuration

 PsysPowerLimit2En_0=0x1,PsysPowerLimit2Power_0=0x3500,PsysPowerLimit2Time_0=0x43 #Psys socket0 PPL2 configuration

tcd_title: Psys RAPL Registers & Reporting: CSR & TPMI
desc:
Flow *


Refer to the OOB/TPMI team's scripts/functions to access each register.


The following are the TPMI Rapl registers of interest:


PLATFORM_RAPL_DOMAIN_HEADER

PLATFORM_RAPL_POWER_UNIT

PLATFORM_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL

PLATFORM_RAPL_PL2_CONTROL

PLATFORM_RAPL_PERF_STATUS

PLATFORM_RAPL_PL_INFO

PLATFORM_RAPL_DOMAIN_INFO




Dynamic RAPL Registers (which will dynamic after boot)

PLATFORM_RAPL_ENERGY_STATUS


Using the above energy status register, calculate the power across each domain. To calculate the power, use dE/dT (where dE = E2 - E1, dT = T2 - T1)


Configuration 

For JOHNSONCITY boards no configuration is required. For the system with customized boards, based upon the sensor configured, make even/odd sockets set as root



Pass/Fail criteria 

In case of static register, the data read via in-band and out-of-band should be the same.


While trying to the register via out of band, The Response code should be successful. (0x40)

In case of dynamic register, the power calculated via In Band and Out of band should be the same and also the power should be a non zero value.

desc: [NWP ZBB] Platform RAPL / Psys is NOT supported on NWP per NWP PM MAS §3 (HSD 22021155415). PPL1/PPL2 are Platform Power Limits (Psys domain). Rejected for NWP.

Validation Scope

Validate Fast RAPL - PEM Verification on NWP silicon/emulation. Feature tag: Socket RAPL.

HAS Ref — Fast RAPL: Fast RAPL updates power limits in a sub-interval cycle (<1 ms). Key registers: fast_rapl_limit, fast_rapl_enable. PEM and PLR are the primary observability paths; HPM messages carry Fast RAPL perf-limit reasons.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
FeatureFast RAPL - PEM Verification
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with Socket RAPL and PM flow enabled.
Automation commandpython runPmx.py -x dmr.xml -p cpu_rapl -tM 6 -M 3
Category / noteActive PM
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Boot to OS. Enable Fast RAPL and run workload to push package power toward the Fast RAPL limit.Fast RAPL engages; PEM records excursion events when power exceeds limit.
2Read PEM registers and confirm Fast RAPL-sourced perf-limit reason present.PEM bit for Fast RAPL set; excursion count non-zero; no non-RAPL fault contamination.
3Run python runPmx.py -x dmr.xml -p cpu_rapl -tM 6 -M 3 end-to-end.Automation PASS; PEM-based Fast RAPL verification complete.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • PEM excursion count non-zero when RAPL throttle active; excursion duration consistent with limit window.
  • Fast RAPL sub-interval enforcement visible; no interval boundary overshoot.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • See Validation Scope for spec / bug references.

tc_title: RACL - CHARGE_STATUS PMT counter Verification per each IMH
desc:
The charge status register reports the accumulated consumed charge of VCCIN_0 and VCCIN_1 domains. This register is updated by prime code and is read-only by all other SW. We need 1 register per die or per IMH; i.e. there will be 2 instances of each register. Verify the functionality of this register.


The below prints two samples racl charges status to make sure it is increementing.

import pm.pmutils.racl as racl

racl.racl_charge_status()
